mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate sits at 0.07%, an effective zero lower bound, signaling maximal monetary accommodation that compresses the equity discount rate and supports elevated valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: near-zero policy rate / zero lower bound regime
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% but 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of observed days during the period, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points sustained over two consecutive months signals accelerating labor market healing, which lifts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp for two consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: If triggered instances of UNRATE decline show 20-day forward SPX returns not significantly greater than the unconditional base rate (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation rises above 4%, markets anticipate accelerated monetary policy tightening, raising the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following CPI YoY crossing above 4% are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a self-reinforcing consumption-employment feedback loop that neither series captures alone, indicating broad-based demand expansion and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 1% AND PAYEMS month-over-month increase exceeds 300,000 jointly in the same reporting month
failure_condition: If joint triggering of RSAFS and PAYEMS growth thresholds fails to produce 20-day forward SPX returns exceeding the unconditional base rate with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct recovery episodes, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding output and improving corporate revenue momentum, which translates into positive short-horizon S&P 500 returns via improving near-term earnings sentiment.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO monthly growth and subsequent 5-day forward SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (Rank IC < 0.02) over any rolling 2-year window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle post-recession recovery regimes, elevated housing starts (HOUST) signal durable-goods-led demand expansion that propagates through construction spending, consumer durables, and employment, producing positive S&P 500 returns with a lag over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its pre-recession trend level and sustains for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST exceeding pre-recession trend are not positive in more than 55% of instances during early-cycle recoveries historically, or Rank IC < 0.03, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed holds rates near zero while unemployment continues declining, markets initially read this as supportive easing; but if CPI inflation simultaneously accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year, markets reprice expectations of imminent tightening, flipping the mechanism from positive to negative for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% AND UNRATE declining month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5%
regime_hint: late accommodation phase with emerging inflation risk
failure_condition: If the joint trigger condition produces positive 20-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the predicted negative direction, or Rank IC magnitude < 0.03, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above a 6% annualized quarter-over-quarter rate, it signals a robust reflationary rebound in aggregate demand that supports continued equity gains over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth above 6% annualized are not positive in more than 55% of historical reflation episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.
